patch.title: Hard Cutover: Behavior-First Rust Testing for Phase 0 Proof

patch.problem_statement: The Rust refactor test suite is not compliant with the new behavior-first testing framework standards. Current proof relies on mixed nextest/unit/integration and proof scripts without standards-required BDD-first scenario execution, mutation-strength gating, and behavior-proxy coverage interpretation. We need a hard cutover that resets Rust test assets and rebuilds them systematically against Phase 0 behaviors while preserving per-task demonstrability via just check and final closure via just ci.

motivations: Enforce standards compliance for Rust testing.
Improve behavior proof quality with positive and falsification witnesses.
Add mutation-strength enforcement to detect weak tests.
Treat coverage as behavior-gap signal rather than vanity metric.
Trial Tanren orchestration in self-host mode and assess readiness.

expectations: Every in-scope Phase 0 behavior maps to stable behavior IDs and executable scenarios.
Each implementation task ends with just check green and demonstrable stage output.
just ci remains the final full gate for spec completion.
Mutation and coverage gates are first-class and blocking at final enforcement.
Rust test cutover leaves no unmanaged legacy behavior-test path.
Meta-evaluation artifact captures orchestration friction and readiness findings.

planned_behaviors: PB-01 Standards baseline includes Rust testing standards and relevance context for this spec.
PB-02 Phase 0 behavior inventory maps Feature 1.1 through 8.1 to stable behavior IDs and scenarios.
PB-03 Scenario files (.feature) become the authoritative behavior proof path.
PB-04 Hard cutover deletes legacy Rust test suite and rebuilds incrementally by behavior waves.
PB-05 Nextest remains required for support/test-binary discipline alongside BDD scenarios.
PB-06 Mutation gate runs in CI/local; survivors require behavior-ID-linked triage.
PB-07 Coverage gate reports missing behavior vs dead/support code classification.
PB-08 Skip suppression is enforced for Rust behavior suites.
PB-09 justfile, pre-commit, CI workflows, and check-ci-parity remain synchronized.
PB-10 Phase 0 proof docs/scripts/evidence index reflect new testing flow.
PB-11 Orchestration trial outputs a meta-readiness report with actionable recommendations.

implementation_plan: Sync/inject Rust testing standards into active standards set.
Define behavior inventory and traceability schema for Phase 0 behaviors.
Introduce gate scaffolding early so each task can prove function and keep just check green.
Scaffold cucumber-rs harness and execute initial vertical slices.
Execute hard-cut milestone deleting legacy Rust behavior tests.
Rebuild behavior coverage in waves until all in-scope behaviors are proven.
Wire mutation and coverage gates with behavior-linked triage/classification artifacts.
Finalize strict enforcement in justfile/lefthook/CI/parity once suites are stable.
Refresh Phase 0 proof scripts/docs/evidence mapping for the new framework.
Produce meta-readiness report on Tanren command orchestration trial.

items: Scope is Rust test replacement only; Python legacy deletion is out of scope for this spec.
No long-lived dual old/new Rust behavior-test stacks.
Each task must end with just check green plus stage-specific demonstrable output.
just ci is the final spec gate before closure.
No skipped behavior scenarios or ignore-based suppression in the cutover suite.
Every behavior-changing claim must map to behavior IDs and scenarios.
Mutation regressions fail at final enforced gate.
Coverage interpretation remains behavior-first with explicit classification.
Gate parity across local hooks and CI must be preserved.
Meta-readiness output is mandatory for this trial run.

criterion.description: Active standards include Rust testing rules for BDD, mutation, coverage, no-skip, and traceability.
criterion.measurable: tanren/standards contains testing standards and relevance filters include this spec scope.

criterion.description: Behavior inventory maps all selected Phase 0 behaviors to stable IDs and scenario files.
criterion.measurable: Traceability artifact lists Feature 1.1-8.1 mappings with scenario references.

criterion.description: Scenario suite provides positive and falsification witnesses for each mapped behavior.
criterion.measurable: Each mapped behavior has at least one passing positive and one passing falsification scenario.

criterion.description: Hard cutover removes legacy Rust behavior-test paths and documents the post-cutover allowed test forms.
criterion.measurable: Legacy Rust tests are deleted from agreed paths and migration policy doc is updated.

criterion.description: justfile exposes staged behavior/mutation/coverage commands and final strict gates.
criterion.measurable: just recipes run successfully at intended stage and are referenced by CI parity checks.

criterion.description: Each task demonstrates functional progress and ends with just check green.
criterion.measurable: Task evidence includes stage command output plus successful just check run.

criterion.description: Mutation gate is blocking at final enforcement with survivor triage tied to behavior IDs.
criterion.measurable: CI/job failure occurs on regression and survivor records reference BEH-* IDs.

criterion.description: Coverage output includes classification of uncovered paths.
criterion.measurable: Coverage report includes missing-behavior vs dead/support code categories.

criterion.description: lefthook, GitHub workflows, and check-ci-parity validate the updated test model.
criterion.measurable: Local hooks and workflow commands pass parity guard with no drift.

criterion.description: Phase 0 proof scripts/docs are updated and reproducible with new test framework.
criterion.measurable: Runbook and evidence index commands regenerate expected proof artifacts.

criterion.description: Meta-evaluation records orchestration trial outcomes and recommendations.
criterion.measurable: A committed report captures strengths, friction points, and go/no-go guidance.

patch.description: Run final just ci and verify full strict gate closure.

patch.description: Execute remote matrix verification step for workflow parity.

T01 standards sync complete: 5 rust-testing standards (bdd-scenarios, mutation-strength, coverage-behavior-first, no-skip-enforcement, behavior-traceability) added under tanren/standards/rust-testing/ and discoverable via standard.list. just check green.

score.rationale: T01 acceptance criteria satisfied: five rust-testing standards were added and do-task evidence includes a successful just check run.
Task adds small markdown standards only; no runtime path or performance-sensitive code changed.
Standards are declarative and apply through pattern matching; no scale-limiting implementation introduced.
Standards capture explicit invariants (BEH tagging, no-skip, mutation triage, coverage classification) without ambiguous task behavior.
No secrets handling or boundary code changed; standards reinforce safer behavior-proof discipline without expanding attack surface.
No executable runtime logic changed in T01; just check passed for the repository state after task implementation evidence.
Standards are split into focused documents with clear category and metadata, improving ongoing maintenance clarity.
New standards are modular by topic and can be extended independently as later tasks operationalize the testing framework.
Implementation uses minimal, direct additions aligned with task scope: standards content only, no unnecessary scaffolding.
Markdown standard files follow established frontmatter and prose conventions used by existing tanren standards.
Changes are scoped to T01 intent: syncing rust-testing standards and recording just check evidence.
Each concern (BDD, mutation, coverage, no-skip, traceability) is isolated in its own standard file with clear boundaries.
Task output is documentation-first and complete for its stated scope; each required standard topic is present.

outcome.summary: T01 audit complete: no fix_now findings; all 13 task-scope pillars scored 10/10 with standards sync evidence and just check passing.

Completed T03 by adding non-blocking staged scenario/mutation/coverage gate scaffolding in justfile, wiring rust-ci and parity checks, documenting staged commands, and verifying with just check-phase0-stage-gates + just check.

score.rationale: T03 acceptance criteria are satisfied: staged scenario/mutation/coverage scaffold commands were added, wired into CI parity surfaces, documented, and evidenced by check-phase0-stage-gates plus just check runs.
New checks are lightweight metadata/tool-availability probes in scaffold mode and do not add meaningful runtime burden beyond deterministic command invocations.
The staged gate entry points separate scenario, mutation, and coverage concerns, enabling independent hardening in later tasks without redesigning orchestration surfaces.
Scaffold checks are intentionally non-blocking per task scope and are explicitly marked as staged, preserving policy strictness by deferring hard enforcement to the dedicated cutover task.
No new privilege boundaries or secret handling were introduced; changes are limited to local/CI command wiring and documentation.
The task records successful execution of check-phase0-stage-gates, check-ci-parity, and just check, indicating the scaffold integration preserved repository stability.
Gate logic is split into small, named just targets with parity assertions and runbook documentation, which keeps future maintenance localized and clear.
Separate staged targets provide clean extension points for future enforcement logic, including replacing scaffold messages with real BDD/mutation/coverage validations.
Implementation is minimal and purpose-built: one justfile scaffold set, one CI hook, one parity guard update, and one runbook update aligned to task intent.
Shell snippets, YAML steps, and Python parity constants follow existing project conventions and naming patterns for quality gates.
All touched files directly support the non-blocking staged gate objective for Phase 0 and avoid unrelated behavior changes.
Scenario, mutation, and coverage scaffolds are isolated into dedicated targets and orchestrated by a composite target, improving modular composition.
Runbook documentation now includes staged gate commands, their current non-blocking behavior, and their role in the ongoing cutover plan.

outcome.summary: T03 audit complete: staged scenario/mutation/coverage scaffolding is correctly wired across justfile, CI workflow, parity checks, and runbook documentation, with no fix_now findings.

T03 adherence complete: no relevant standards matched the task evidence files (justfile, .github/workflows/rust-ci.yml, scripts/check_ci_parity.py, docs/rewrite/PHASE0_PROOF_RUNBOOK.md); no fix_now findings recorded.

score.rationale: T04 acceptance criteria are met: the cucumber-rs harness runs a tagged smoke scenario and the task evidence records successful just check-phase0-bdd-smoke and just check runs.
The scaffold adds a single lightweight smoke execution path and introduces no runtime production hot-path changes.
The harness and tests/bdd/phase0 conventions provide a clear path to add more feature files and behavior scenarios incrementally.
The harness enforces fail_on_skipped and the smoke gate fails fast on missing feature/tag evidence, tightening execution invariants for staged BDD runs.
Changes are limited to test harness and documentation surfaces with no new network, auth, or secrets-handling attack surface.
The smoke world is deterministic with max_concurrent_scenarios(1), and do-task evidence includes a passing full just check run.
BDD scaffold responsibilities are split cleanly across an isolated crate, feature assets, and concise runbook/justfile updates.
Directory and tagging conventions in tests/bdd/phase0 establish a reusable contract for later wave migrations.
Implementation is minimal and focused: one small harness crate, one smoke feature, and one staged gate command.
Rust, justfile, and documentation updates follow existing repository idioms and naming conventions.
Touched files align directly with T04 scope: harness scaffold, feature conventions, smoke execution, and related proof/runbook wiring.
The new tanren-bdd-phase0 crate keeps BDD harness concerns isolated from core runtime and service crates.
T04 includes explicit documentation updates in tests/bdd/phase0/README.md and PHASE0_PROOF_RUNBOOK.md for usage and staged-gate behavior.

outcome.summary: T04 audit complete: no fix_now findings; all task-scope pillars scored passing based on cucumber-rs harness scaffold evidence, smoke-tag execution proof, and recorded just check success.

T04 adherence complete: evaluated relevant rust-error-handling, observability, security, style, and rust-testing standards against the implemented scaffold artifacts (main.rs, smoke.feature, README, justfile/runbook references); no fix_now adherence findings recorded.

The harness enforces fail_on_skipped and step logic asserts typed rejection paths for invalid lifecycle transitions, invalid replay input, and invalid auth or replay credentials.
Wave A includes explicit auth and replay falsification scenarios for protected commands, and no new secrets or privilege boundary surface was introduced.
Task evidence includes successful check-phase0-stage-gates and just check execution, indicating the added Wave A scenarios integrate without destabilizing the repository checks.
Behavior steps are centralized in tanren-bdd-phase0 while feature files remain focused per feature, keeping maintenance localized and readable.

score.rationale: T06 acceptance criteria are met: legacy proof ownership paths were retired from active use and post-cutover allowed forms are documented in policy plus proof docs.

The retired script now fails closed with explicit guidance, reducing ambiguous legacy behavior-proof entry points.
No new external interfaces or secret-handling surfaces were introduced; the change narrows accepted behavior-proof mechanisms.
Do-task evidence records a successful just check run after the cutover updates, indicating repository checks remained stable.
Cutover intent is captured in a dedicated policy document and aligned runbook/index updates, improving operational clarity.
The documented post-cutover model leaves clear seams for later wave tasks while preserving deterministic command entry points.
Implementation is direct: retire legacy ownership path, define allowed forms, and align core proof documentation with minimal surface area.
Updated markdown and shell messaging follow existing repository conventions and keep wording consistent with Phase 0 terminology.
Touched artifacts are tightly scoped to T06 goals: hard cutover of legacy behavior-proof ownership and allowed-form documentation.
Behavior ownership guidance is isolated in dedicated policy and BDD docs rather than spread across code paths, preserving boundary clarity.
Task-scope documentation is complete for the cutover: policy, proof evidence index, runbook, and BDD conventions are all updated.

outcome.summary: T06 audit complete: no fix_now findings. Hard cutover changes retire legacy run.sh behavior ownership, document allowed post-cutover forms, and retain passing task-scope rubric scores across all 13 pillars.

Wave B adds deterministic BDD scenarios and lightweight in-memory step logic only, without introducing runtime hot-path work or expensive operations.

Wave B follows the same BEH-tagged positive/falsification pattern as prior waves, preserving a reusable contract for future feature migrations.

Feature naming, tag patterns, and step function conventions align with existing phase0 BDD style and repository command naming patterns.

Wave B behavior checks are isolated from core runtime crates via the tanren-bdd-phase0 harness and per-feature files, preserving domain boundary clarity.
tests/bdd/phase0/README.md and traceability mappings cover Wave B command usage and BEH obligations, with phase events recording verification evidence.

outcome.summary: T07 audit complete: no fix_now findings. Wave B scenario migration for Features 4-6 is traceable to BEH-P0-401/402/501/502/601/602/603 with positive/falsification witnesses, typed step support, stage-gate wiring, and successful just check evidence.

T08 complete: Wave C Feature 7/8 BDD scenarios implemented with passing stage-gate evidence and just check green.

score.rationale: Wave C scope is fully covered: Feature 7 (BEH-P0-701/702/703) and Feature 8 (BEH-P0-801) each include positive and falsification scenarios with matching step bindings and stage-gate wiring.
Changes are test-harness and feature-definition additions only; no production runtime hot-path behavior or resource-heavy logic was introduced.
Wave C follows the existing per-feature and per-wave partitioning pattern, keeping future scenario growth incremental and low-coupling.
Scenarios assert explicit deterministic outcomes and explicit failure conditions (drift detection, semantic parity drift, and loop-coherence breakdown) with no ambiguous pass criteria.
No new secret-handling or trust-boundary code was introduced; behavior checks reinforce typed-tool mutation flow and explicit denial of unsafe mutation paths.
Task evidence records successful just check-phase0-stage-gates and just check runs after these changes, supporting stable integration at task scope.
Wave C logic is isolated in a dedicated wave_c_steps module with clear, behavior-aligned step functions and feature files.
The implementation preserves the established BEH-tagged positive/falsification pattern, making additional Phase 0 behavior scenarios straightforward to add.
Implementation is minimal and targeted: one module registration, one new step file, two feature files, and one stage-gate recipe addition.
Naming, tagging, and command conventions match existing phase0 BDD style (feature filenames, @BEH tags, and just recipe structure).
All touched artifacts directly support T08 intent: Feature 7 installer/self-host determinism and Feature 8 manual walkthrough coherence scenarios.
Behavior verification remains contained in the tanren-bdd-phase0 harness and feature files, preserving boundaries from production domain modules.
Wave C command usage is documented in tests/bdd/phase0/README.md and traceability mappings include BEH-P0-701/702/703/801 to their feature files.

T08 adherence complete: evaluated relevant rust-error-handling, observability, security, style, and rust-testing standards against Wave C evidence files (main.rs, wave_c_steps.rs, feature-7/8, README, justfile); no fix_now adherence findings recorded.

T09 complete: replaced mutation scaffold with staged cargo-mutants gate runner, emitted machine-readable Phase 0 mutation triage artifact with BEH-ID linkage, wired CI install+artifact upload and parity checks, updated proof runbook/evidence index, and verified with just check.

score.rationale: T09 acceptance criteria are met: mutation stage command is wired in local staged gates and CI, and it emits machine-consumable triage JSON linked to Phase 0 BEH IDs with just check passing evidence.
Changes are confined to test/proof scripts, CI wiring, and documentation; no production runtime hot-path behavior was introduced.
Mutation execution uses shard and timeout controls and writes timestamped artifact directories, supporting incremental scaling and parallelized staged runs.
The staged mutation flow enforces deterministic command shape, explicit run status, and survivor triage policy with BEH linkage requirements, while clearly marking non-blocking staged semantics.

Updated spawn_without_runtime_spec_folder helper to env_remove TANREN_SPEC_FOLDER and TANREN_SPEC_ID

payload.resolution: Cleared inherited runtime env in the no-runtime helper (env_remove TANREN_SPEC_FOLDER/TANREN_SPEC_ID); subsequent just ci passed fully with strict mutation gate survivors=0.

score.rationale: Task evidence closes the reported mutation-gap objective: previously missed submit_lifecycle_transition mutants are now caught in enforced mutation artifacts, and the hermetic runtime-env test guard fix removes the false-negative CI blocker.
The implementation is test-only and limited to environment cleanup in process spawn setup, introducing no runtime performance regression in production code paths.
Removing inherited runtime env from the helper makes mutation-runtime validation behavior consistent across local and orchestrated environments, reducing environment-size dependent drift.
The no-runtime-path validation contract is now exercised under explicitly clean env conditions, preserving strict env-derived scope checks expected by the tool surface.
Clearing inherited spec env for the negative-path helper reduces accidental cross-session context bleed in tests and keeps validation outcomes tied to explicit runtime inputs.
The change hardens test hermeticity and removes flaky dependence on ambient shell environment, improving repeatability of the mutation-runtime guard assertions.
The fix is localized to spawn_without_runtime_spec_folder with two explicit env removals, making intent and ownership clear without broad refactors.
A hermetic helper now provides a reliable base for future negative-path mutation-runtime tests that need clean env preconditions.
The remediation uses the smallest viable change set to address the root cause (ambient env leakage) while keeping existing test structure intact.
The patch follows existing Command builder chaining and naming conventions in the test module.
Touched code and recorded evidence are directly scoped to T14 goals: mutation-gate remediation evidence and runtime-env guard reliability.
Environment hygiene is contained in the spawn helper abstraction, avoiding cross-cutting changes to unrelated tests or runtime services.
Task evidence refs include the updated mutation-runtime test plus enforced mutation and coverage classification artifacts, and signpost resolution records the root cause and fix.
